content.text:
<user_query>
Review requested update: in ~/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh interactive conflict prompt, add explicit per-conflict user prompt including an option to select the most recently edited file as winner. Check for compatibility with existing conflict report schema (entries include mtime), decision output format, non-interactive mode expectations, and any UX/accessibility risks. Flag regressions and recommend robust logic for tie timestamps and unreachable destinations.
</user_query>

yes. The current `sync_toolbox.sh` flow can support a “most recently edited” option, but there are clear compatibility risks and one hard failure risk if it’s added without schema-aware changes.

Findings:
- High — Conflict objects in the report do not carry per-source entry metadata, so a new prompt-level “most recent” choice cannot read mtime from `conflicts` directly. `build_conflict_report` only stores `id/category/relpath/present_sources/missing_sources/hash_groups` under each conflict, while per-source `mtime` exists only inside `files[].entries`. `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:375-383` and `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:385-393`.
- Medium — Non-interactive compatibility is strict on source identity; `build_operation_plan` treats any resolution choice as invalid unless it matches a `present_source`. If you record a symbolic choice token such as `recent`, `most-recent`, or `auto`, it will become an `invalid_source` skip even though interactive UX selected it. `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:760-766` and `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:712-717`.
- Medium — Unreachable destinations can be silently ignored when requested via `--destinations`: filtering allows them (in `all_sources`) and then no operation is generated because only `reachable` destinations are iterated. This can make a user believe sync is attempted while no destination receives work. `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:726-733`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:768-780`.
- Low — The UX prompt currently lists only source names and indices; adding “recent winner” is a good improvement, but tie/ambiguity handling is still absent and must be explicit to avoid nondeterministic behavior when mtimes are equal. See current prompt options block and selection loop. `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:652-661` and `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:663-677`.
- Low — Current interactive non-tty behavior is brittle by design: resolve logic assumes an interactive answer stream exists and does not provide a machine-readable “no destination” path from interactive mode, so it is easy for automation to get stuck/abort unexpectedly. `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:1015-1021` and `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:663-677`.

Recommended robust logic:
- Resolve recency from the existing schema by joining `conflict.id` to `files` entries (where `mtime` exists), then compute max by a stable key: `(mtime, source_index_in_all_sources)`; never rely on arbitrary dict ordering.
- Keep decision output backward compatible: map “use most recent” to an actual source before writing `decisions[].choice`, so `decisions_out` remains `{id, choice}` and TSV conversion stays unchanged. `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:441-459`, `/Users/HanHu/.cursor/scripts/sync_toolbox.sh:679-690`.
- Handle tie timestamps deterministically and visibly: when mtime ties, use deterministic source precedence and display the chosen tie-break rule to the user in the prompt.
- Add explicit unreachable destination handling in plan building: if requested destination is known but not reachable, emit `skipped` with a dedicated reason (`destination_unreachable`) or fail fast before operation generation.

If you want, I can draft a minimal patch to this single script that adds the “most recent” option with tie-break logic and unreachable-destination skip visibility while preserving current JSON/TSV decision formats.

done
